briefdescription.para: Callback type for vendor handling of HCI Vendor-Specific Events.

detaileddescription.para: A function of this type is registered with bt_hci_register_vnd_evt_cb() and will be called for any HCI Vendor-Specific Event.

simplesect.para: true if the function handles the event or false to defer the handling of this event back to the stack.

briefdescription.para: Converts a HCI error to string.

detaileddescription.para: The error codes are described in the Bluetooth Core specification, Vol 1, Part F, Section 2.
The HCI documentation found in Vol 4, Part E, describes when the different error codes are used.
See also the defined BT_HCI_ERR_* macros.

simplesect.para: The string representation of the HCI error code. If CONFIG_BT_HCI_ERR_TO_STR is not enabled, this just returns the empty string

detaileddescription.para: This function allocates a new buffer for an HCI command. Upon successful return the buffer is ready to have the command parameters encoded into it. Sufficient headroom gets automatically reserved in the buffer, allowing the actual command and H:4 headers to be encoded later, as part of calling bt_hci_cmd_send() or bt_hci_cmd_send_sync().

simplesect.para: Newly allocated buffer or NULL if allocation failed.

detaileddescription.para: This function is used for sending a HCI command asynchronously. It can either be called for a buffer created using bt_hci_cmd_alloc(), or if the command has no parameters a NULL can be passed instead. The sending of the command will happen asynchronously, i.e. upon successful return from this function the caller only knows that it was queued successfully.
If synchronous behavior, and retrieval of the Command Complete parameters is desired, the bt_hci_cmd_send_sync() API should be used instead.

parameterdescription.para: Command buffer or NULL (if no parameters).

simplesect.para: 0 on success or negative error value on failure.

detaileddescription.para: This function is used for sending a HCI command synchronously. It can either be called for a buffer created using bt_hci_cmd_alloc(), or if the command has no parameters a NULL can be passed instead.

The function will block until a Command Status or a Command Complete event is returned. If either of these have a non-zero status the function will return a negative error code and the response reference will not be set. If the command completed successfully and a non-NULL rsp parameter was given, this parameter will be set to point to a buffer containing the response parameters.

parameterdescription.para: Command buffer or NULL (if no parameters).

Place to store a reference to the command response. May be NULL if the caller is not interested in the response parameters. If non-NULL is passed the caller is responsible for calling net_buf_unref() on the buffer when done parsing it.

simplesect.para: 0 on success or negative error value on failure.

briefdescription.para: Get connection handle for a connection.

parameterdescription.para: Place to store the Connection handle.

simplesect.para: 0 on success or negative error value on failure.

briefdescription.para: Get connection given a connection handle.

detaileddescription.para: The caller gets a new reference to the connection object which must be released with bt_conn_unref() once done using the object.

simplesect.para: The corresponding connection object on success. NULL if it does not exist.

briefdescription.para: Get advertising handle for an advertising set.

parameterdescription.para: Place to store the advertising handle.

simplesect.para: 0 on success or negative error value on failure.

briefdescription.para: Get advertising set given an advertising handle.

simplesect.para: The corresponding advertising set on success, NULL if it does not exist.

parameterdescription.para: Place to store the periodic advertising sync handle.

simplesect.para: 0 on success or negative error value on failure.

briefdescription.para: Get periodic advertising sync given an periodic advertising sync handle.

simplesect.para: The corresponding periodic advertising sync set object on success, NULL if it does not exist.

briefdescription.para: Obtain the version string given a core version number.

detaileddescription.para: The core version of a controller can be obtained by issuing the HCI Read Local Version Information command.
See also the defines prefixed with BT_HCI_VERSION_.

simplesect.para: Version string corresponding to the core version number.

briefdescription.para: Register user callback for HCI Vendor-Specific Events.

parameterdescription.para: Callback to be called when the stack receives a HCI Vendor-Specific Event.

simplesect.para: 0 on success or negative error value on failure.

briefdescription.para: Get Random bytes from the LE Controller.

detaileddescription.para: Send the HCI_LE_Rand to the LE Controller as many times as required to fill the provided buffer.

simplesect.para: This function is provided as a helper to gather an arbitrary number of random bytes from an LE Controller using the HCI_LE_Rand command.

parameterdescription.para: Buffer to fill with random bytes.
Length of the buffer in bytes.

simplesect.para: 0 on success or negative error value on failure.
